title: Iliahia pahulu Austin & Rubinoff 2026
author: Austin, Kyhl A.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-zero
description: Iliahia pahulu  Austin & Rubinoff, sp. nov.   http://zoobank.org/ urn:lsid:zoobank.org:act: E8289CDB-4987-4641-AD1B-5162622A2A7C  Figs 1G, H; 2C, 3C   Diagnosis.   Iliahia pahulu  is most similar to  I .  santalata  (Swezey), but can be distinguished by the darker HW (white or pale gray in  I .  santalata ) and slender and thorn-like process from the disc of the valvae in males (semicircular and flap-like in  I .  santalata ).   Type material.   Holotype,  ♂.  Lānaʻi:  Munro Trail; 20.8126, -156.8731; 1025 m; 8–9 i 2025; K. A. Austin, S. L. Pote, C. Doorenweerd; UV bucket trap / DNA extraction KA1431 / KAA diss. #1377 (UHIM).  Paratype  (1♀).  Lānaʻi:  1♀, Munro Trail; 2 x 1976 / K. & E. Sattler / KAA diss. #1400 (NHMUK).   Description. Male (n=1).     Head .  Frons clothed in long, porrect pale yellow scales, a thin row of red-orange scales laterally; vertex with long, pale yellow scales, pale brown and pale red-orange scales laterally and around scape; lateral surface of labial palpus with first and second segment with intermixed brown and red-orange scales, a few yellow scales at apex of second segment; third segment with scaling predominantly pale yellow; medial surface pale yellow. Scape with scales on dorsal half red-orange and brown, pale yellow ventrally; dorsal scales of flagellum with basal 0.25× alternating rows of pale yellow and red-orange (entirely pale yellow on ventral half), alternating rows of brown and pale yellow scales beyond.  Thorax.  Scales on pronotum mostly missing, but some pale yellow scales present at anterolateral and extreme posterior margin, a small patch of brown scales anteriad of the latter; tegulae red-orange at base, pale yellow at apex. Foreleg entirely brown except for red-orange coxa. Midleg pale grey, tarsus pale yellow. Hindleg pale yellow, nearly white. Dorsal surface of FW (FWL: 5.2 mm; Fig. 1G) with ground color pale yellow, overlaid with a network of brown-red strigulae, especially apparent along basal half of FW; FW costal fold slender, brown-red and red-orange, interrupted medially by a pale yellow spot; subapical blotch divided into three similarsized spots, the costal pair triangular and pale yellow, the spot at the end of cell white; basal fascia pale yellow at base, brownish-red beyond; median fascia brownish-red, suffused with gray scales (possibly iridescent when fresh), fused with tornal blotch and through subapical blotch, extending to fringe; fringe pale yellow. Dorsal surface of HW dark gray. Ventral surface of FW entirely dark gray except for two pale yellow spots on costa at subapical blotch. Ventral surface of HW similar to dorsal surface but slightly paler.   Abdomen .  Vestiture of abdomen dark gray, ventral scaling pale brownish-yellow. Genitalia (Fig. 2C) with uncus shovel-shaped, triangular, slightly thickened on lateral margin, apically acute, nearly confluent with tegumen; socii obsolete; tegumen high, arched; gnathos moderate, arms laterally compressed, fused beyond 0.75 × to form plate, apically acute; transtilla with labides inflated laterally, not spined, joined medially by thin membrane; valvae short, irregular, slightly ovoid, apically rounded; sacculus well-developed, to 0.75× valva, relatively broad, with a distinct, short, thorn-like process projecting into disc of valve from end of sacculus; juxta well-sclerotized, deeply inset, with a shallow ventral trough; phallus moderate, nearly straight except for shallow cavity near ventral base, apically acute, without observed cornuti nor sockets.   Description. Female (n=1).    Head.  Similar to male, but sensillae shorter and more extensive yellow scaling on vertex and base of scape.   Thorax.  Scaling on pronotum similar to male but with diamond-shaped patch of brown scales occupying much of the dorsal surface. FW larger (FWL: 6.2 mm; Fig. 1H), with much more extensive yellow scaling over much of the FW, only faintly overlaid with red-orange scales, subapical patch not subdivided, without white patch at end of cell; median fascia much thinner than in male, tornal blotch slightly thinner than in male. Ventral surface of FW largely brown, but with much larger patches of yellow scales in basal third and at subapical blotch. HW similar to male.   Abdomen .  Vestiture of abdomen similar to male. Genitalia (Fig. 3C) with papillae anales moderate, semicircular, parallel-sided, roughened on ventral surface; apophyses posteriores ca. 0.5× S7, straight, thickened distally; apophyses anteriores ca. 0.8× S7, slightly curved, distally flattened; sterigma moderate, broadly V-shaped, well-sclerotized; ostium ring-like; colliculum faint, scarcely observable; ductus bursae ca. 2.5× S7, straight, of uniform width throughout; ductus seminalis arising near base of ductus bursae; corpus bursae globose, membranous, signum absent.   Etymology.  This species is named in honor of the Hawaiian goddess Pahulu, the leading akua (spirit) of Lānaʻi.   Biology.  Unknown, but larvae are presumed to be leaf-rollers on  Santalum haleakalae  var.  lanaiense  (Santalaceae), the only species of  Santalum  known from Lānaihale.   Distribution.   Iliahia pahulu  is only known from two specimens collected along the Munro Trail near the summit of Lānaihale, Lānaʻi. It is possible that this species also exists (or existed) in similar habitat on Molokaʻi and Mauna ʻEʻeka (west Maui) in the vicinity of  Santalum haleakalae  var.  lanaiense  on those islands.   Conservation status.  Likely critically endangered. Only ca. 30 trees of  Santalum haleakalae  var.  lanaiense  are known to still remain on Lānaʻi (USFWS 2023). Surveys on Molokaʻi and Mauna ʻEʻeka (west Maui) are badly needed to see if  Iliahia pahulu  occurs there in the event it or its presumed host go extinct on Lānaʻi.
